title: asperula purpurea var. trichantha
intro: Asperula purpurea var. trichantha, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. Woodruff can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a flavoring agent in various culinary and medicinal preparations.
